key_insight: The paper argues that LLMs can evolve from prompt-level cognitive simulation to a native meta-architecture by using structural tension, offline recurrent self-processing, and inference-time plasticity to drive internally consistent, path-dependent topological change.
engineering_relevance: This reframes AI architecture as a governed constitutive system where memory, adaptation, and self-organization are handled at inference time without changing pretrained weights.
method: Define structural tension as an endogenous loss from conflict between new input and existing manifold topology
Run an offline recurrent loop to maintain a resting potential and resolve internal conflicts without external prompts
Apply inference-time plasticity with governance invariants: auditability, reversibility, and topological continuity
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a theoretical framework with operational definitions, reconfiguration operators, falsification criteria, and a worked example, but does not report empirical benchmarks or dataset-based evaluation.
watch_item: The main implementation risk is ensuring that inference-time topology changes remain auditable and reversible while avoiding uncontrolled drift or hidden state accumulation.
paper_title: From Application-Layer Simulation to Native Meta-Architecture: Structural Tension as an Endogenous Driver for Heterogeneous AI Evolution
